paper.title: High Velocity Stars in SDSS/APOGEE DR17

extraction.summary: Three unbound candidates extracted from the HiVel sample. The paper explicitly labels these stars as 'unbound candidates' because they exceed the MWPotential2014 escape velocity, but notes the excess is within 1σ uncertainties and concludes they are bound in the preferred Irrgang Model I potential. The remaining 20 HiVel stars are excluded as the paper concludes they are bound in all potentials.

method_chain.summary: APOGEE DR17 spectroscopic catalog and Gaia eDR3 astrometric catalog

source_refs.context: Data sources: APOGEE DR17 and Gaia eDR3

method_chain.summary: Cross-match APOGEE DR17 with Gaia eDR3 to obtain full 6D phase space

source_refs.context: Complementing APOGEE DR17 with Gaia eDR3 astrometry

method_chain.summary: Quality cuts on APOGEE and Gaia data: SNR>10, VERR<1 km/s, NVISITS>1, VSCATTER<1 km/s, ASPCAPFLAG/STARFLAG cuts, Gaia ruwe<1.4, fidelity_v2>0.5, norm_dG<-3, sigma_v/v_GC<30%

source_refs.context: Quality cuts and sample selection
Photogeometric distances from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021)

method_chain.summary: APOGEE DR17 radial velocities from Doppler pipeline

source_refs.context: APOGEE DR17 radial velocity determination with Doppler algorithm

method_chain.summary: Galactocentric coordinate transformation with 1000 Monte Carlo realizations using Astropy and Pyia

source_refs.context: Transformation from ICRS to Galactocentric coordinates with Monte Carlo uncertainty propagation

method_chain.summary: ASPCAP stellar atmospheric parameters and isochrone-derived masses and radii

source_refs.context: ASPCAP stellar parameter and abundance pipeline

method_chain.summary: Irrgang Model I, McMillan17, and MWPotential2014 gravitational potentials implemented in galpy
outputs: potential models for orbit integration and escape velocity

source_refs.context: Three gravitational potential models for unbound assessment

method_chain.summary: Back-in-time orbit integration with 1000 MC realizations for 10 Gyr in Irrgang Model I potential using galpy

source_refs.context: Orbit integration methodology

method_chain.summary: Unbound probability p_ub = n(v_GC > v_esc)/1000 computed for each potential model

source_refs.context: Unbound candidate identification and probability calculation

method_chain.summary: Chemical tagging with APOGEE abundances and dynamical origin analysis including LMC perturbation

source_refs.context: Chemical patterns and dynamical origin analysis

method_chain.summary: Absolute magnitude determination from Gaia eDR3 photometry, Bailer-Jones distances, and extinction correction

source_refs.context: Absolute magnitudes from Gaia eDR3 with dust extinction correction

inclusion_assessment.summary: Unbound candidate exceeding MWPotential2014 escape velocity. The paper finds it has an 18% probability of passing within 5 kpc of the LMC. Kinematically and chemically consistent with halo stars.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly labels this object as an unbound candidate with respect to the MWPotential2014 model, but concludes it is bound in the preferred Irrgang Model I potential and notes the excess is within 1σ uncertainties.

source_refs.context: Abstract: three stars could be unbound depending on the adopted potential
Unbound candidates section: three stars exceed MWPotential2014 escape velocity
Conclusions: 3 stars unbounded to MWPotential2014 but bounded to Irrgang Model I
Abstract introduces three unbound candidates
Conclusions reassess unbound status

bound_status_metric.description: Orbital energy in the Irrgang Model I potential. Negative value indicates a bound orbit. The paper concludes all HiVel stars are bound in this potential.

source_refs.context: All stars result to have bound orbits in Irrgang Model I

spectroscopy.description: Red Giant Branch classification from Hertzsprung-Russell diagram analysis

source_refs.context: All HiVel stars are located in the Red Giant Branch

mass.description: Mass derived from APOGEE DR17 parameters and MIST isochrones. The paper notes some isochrone masses may be unreliable.

radius.description: Radius derived from APOGEE DR17 parameters and MIST isochrones.

origin_classification.description: Chemical and kinematic analysis suggests the star belongs to the low-alpha halo population of probably accreted stars.

source_refs.context: Most HiVel stars belong to low-alpha halo population of probably accreted stars

hypothesis_metrics.description: Probability of passing within 5 kpc of the LMC during orbit integration including LMC perturbation

source_refs.context: Only star with probability >0.1 of LMC proximity

inclusion_assessment.summary: Unbound candidate exceeding MWPotential2014 escape velocity. Highly enriched in aluminum and nitrogen. The paper notes the isochrone mass is probably wrong and suggests possible accretion from a globular cluster.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly labels this object as an unbound candidate with respect to the MWPotential2014 model, but concludes it is bound in the preferred Irrgang Model I potential and notes the excess is within 1σ uncertainties.

Conclusions: 3 stars unbounded to MWPotential2014 but bounded to Irrgang Model I

other.description: Highly enriched in Al and N, fitting the population of stars probably accreted from globular clusters

source_refs.context: Peculiar case 2M15180013+0209292: enriched in Al and N, possible globular cluster origin

inclusion_assessment.summary: Unbound candidate exceeding MWPotential2014 escape velocity. Metal-poor star with typical halo kinematics and chemistry.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly labels this object as an unbound candidate with respect to the MWPotential2014 model, but concludes it is bound in the preferred Irrgang Model I potential and notes the excess is within 1σ uncertainties.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 20 HiVel stars with Galactocentric velocities >450 km/s that are bound in all adopted potential models.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper concludes these 20 stars are bound to the Galaxy in all potential models considered (Irrgang Model I, McMillan17, MWPotential2014). They are treated as high-velocity halo stars, not as unbound or HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Conclusions: 20 stars display velocities greater than 450 km/s but are all bounded to the Galaxy
Unbound candidates section: only three stars exceed MWPotential2014 escape velocity
